title: Chest CT Pneumonia/COVID Severity Classification (PWDR)
sub_domain: Lung tissue density reconstruction with pneumonia/COVID severity-grade categorical readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 physical_with_discrete_readout (PWDR) genesis Principle. Wraps L1-029 CT (analytical core) with a deterministic threshold function on lobe-segmented ground-glass-opacity / consolidation / crazy-paving fractions to yield CO-RADS / Total Severity Score (TSS) categorical grades. Universal clinical method during pneumonia surveillance (community-acquired pneumonia, hospital-acquired, ventilator-associated, COVID-19 acute and post-acute). Validated and accepted globally including UTSW.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on TSS-grade {0=normal, 1=<5%, 2=5-25%, 3=26-50%, 4=51-75%, 5=>75%} or CO-RADS {1-5} ordinal readout

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Total Severity Score TSS(lung) = sum_lobe TSS_lobe where TSS_lobe = grade_k such that GGO_consolidation_fraction_lobe in [tau_k, tau_{k+1}); tau_thresholds = [0%, 5%, 25%, 50%, 75%] per Pan/Yang 2020 + Francone 2020. CO-RADS(lung) = 1-5 categorical likelihood per Prokop 2020 Dutch radiology consensus, mapped from morphological pattern features (peripheral vs central distribution, multifocal vs unifocal, GGO vs consolidation predominance, crazy-paving presence).
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: lung tissue density distribution mu_attenuation(r) [HU] reconstructed from CT projections via L1-029 CT; lobe-segmented GGO/consolidation/normal fractions per region; morphological pattern descriptors
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: TSS-grade is a step function on per-lobe involvement-fraction (continuous in [0,1]); each tau_k boundary is a measure-zero hyperplane. Smoothed-grading is globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.05 per percentage-point of involvement fraction. Inherits underlying-state stability from L1-029 CT.

related_principles: L1-029 (CT — tissue-density reconstruction analytical core, physics_core_principle)

E.description: Chest CT Severity Classification (PWDR): wraps L1-029 CT analytical core with the international Pan/Francone Total Severity Score and Dutch CO-RADS clinical-grading rules. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-029): reconstruct lung tissue density mu_attenuation(r) [HU] from CT projections; segment lobes and identify abnormal regions per Hounsfield-density thresholds (-700 to -400 HU = ground-glass opacity GGO; -100 to 50 HU = consolidation; -700 to -800 + interlobular septal thickening = crazy-paving). Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): aggregate per-lobe involvement fractions to per-lobe TSS sub-scores; sum across 5 lobes for total TSS in [0, 25]; apply morphological-pattern rules to yield CO-RADS likelihood category. The threshold function is piecewise-constant on per-lobe involvement fractions and locally Lipschitz-continuous on the underlying density reconstruction. Difficulty tier delta = 3 inherited from L1-029. Mismatch parameters: kV_calibration_drift, slice_thickness_anisotropy, motion_blur_breath_hold, contrast_enhancement_state, lobe_segmentation_error, threshold_calibration_uncertainty.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-029): {y_projection(theta, s)} -> mu_attenuation(r). Stage 2 (TSS / CO-RADS): GGO_fraction_lobe = volume(mu in GGO_HU_range) / volume_lobe; TSS_lobe = step(GGO_fraction_lobe at tau_thresholds); TSS_total = sum_lobe TSS_lobe; CO-RADS = morphological_classifier(GGO_distribution, consolidation_distribution, crazy_paving_presence, peripheral_vs_central_ratio).
E.world_state_x: Per-patient TSS in [0, 25] and CO-RADS in {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}; optionally per-lobe TSS sub-scores.
E.observation_y: Standard non-contrast or contrast-enhanced chest CT volume (typically 512x512 in-plane, 100-500 axial slices, 1-3 mm slice thickness).

G.dag: L.xray_source -> L.attenuation_projection -> L.filtered_back_projection -> L.lobe_segmentation -> L.density_thresholding -> L.tss_classifier -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: lobe_to_density: lobe-segmentation and density-thresholding share the same reconstructed CT volume; coupled processing enforces topological consistency (involvement fractions are evaluated within anatomically-defined lobe boundaries)

W.regime: Existence and uniqueness inherited from L1-029 CT. Stability inherits L1-029's well-conditioned reconstruction plus a small additive contribution from threshold_calibration_uncertainty. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled CT-reconstruction + TSS/CO-RADS forward established by Pan F et al. (2020, foundational TSS), Francone M et al. (2020 TSS validation), Prokop M et al. (2020 CO-RADS Dutch consensus), Inui S et al. (2020 multicenter TSS), Liu Z et al. (2021 deep-learning TSS automation), and Lessmann N et al. (2021 multicentre CO-RADS validation).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [L1-029 FBP + lobe segmentation] + categorical-readout [TSS/CO-RADS rule] | end-to-end deep neural [DeepSARS, COVNet, COVID-Net] with explicit physics-informed lobe-segmentation regularization
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-029 reconstruction bound; classification stage adds C_thresh * |involvement_fraction_error| / d(fraction, tau_boundary). Achieves q=2 convergence on reconstruction, classification stage inherits with grader-calibration floor.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * log(N)) for stage 1 (inherited from L1-029); O(N_lobes * N_HU_classes) for stage 2 thresholding; total stage-1-dominated

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Classify chest CT into severity grades' (would be data_driven_statistical CNN). Option-3 recast: 'Reconstruct lung tissue density via L1-029 CT; segment lobes and identify GGO/consolidation per Hounsfield-density thresholds; apply Pan/Francone TSS or Prokop CO-RADS rules to involvement fractions'. Feasible because lung density has a well-defined CT forward model and TSS/CO-RADS are deterministic clinical-rule functions of recovered density distributions.

joint_well_posedness_references: Pan F et al. (2020) Time course of lung changes at chest CT during recovery from coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19). Radiology 295(3):715-721.
Francone M et al. (2020) Chest CT score in COVID-19 patients: correlation with disease severity and short-term prognosis. Eur Radiol 30(12):6808-6817.
Prokop M et al. (2020) CO-RADS: a categorical CT assessment scheme for patients suspected of having COVID-19 — definition and evaluation. Radiology 296(2):E97-E104.
Inui S et al. (2020) Chest CT findings in cases from the cruise ship 'Diamond Princess' with coronavirus disease 2019. Radiol Cardiothorac Imaging 2(2):e200110.
Liu Z et al. (2021) An automatic and effective method to identify pneumonia type and severity from chest CT scans. Comput Methods Programs Biomed 207:106177.
Lessmann N et al. (2021) Automated assessment of CO-RADS and chest CT severity scores in patients with suspected COVID-19 using artificial intelligence. Radiology 298(1):E18-E28.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-029 CT recovers tissue attenuation distribution mu(r); L1-514 wraps with TSS / CO-RADS grading rules. Different solution_space (1D severity grade vs 3D density volume); adds L.lobe_segmentation, L.density_thresholding, L.tss_classifier primitives.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal clinical use during pneumonia surveillance — community-acquired pneumonia, hospital-acquired pneumonia, ventilator-associated pneumonia, and importantly COVID-19 acute disease and post-acute sequelae. CO-RADS adopted across European radiology departments since 2020; TSS used globally for severity stratification and prognosis. UTSW Department of Radiology participates in pneumonia and COVID-19 imaging research. PWDR formulation gives physics-grounded explainability: severity is derived from explicit involvement fractions of recovered lung density, not black-box scoring.
